howItWorks.intro: This apr calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: APR accounts for fees by calculating the rate on the net amount received
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is APR?
faq.answer: Annual Percentage Rate includes the interest rate plus fees, giving you the true cost of borrowing. It is always equal to or higher than the stated interest rate.

faq.question: Why is APR different from interest rate?
faq.answer: APR factors in origination fees, closing costs, and other charges. A 6% loan with $500 in fees on a $20,000 loan has an APR higher than 6%.

faq.question: Which is more important, rate or APR?
faq.answer: APR is better for comparing loans because it includes all costs. However, if you plan to refinance or pay off early, the monthly payment matters more.

faq.question: What fees are included in APR?
faq.answer: Origination fees, discount points, mortgage insurance, and some closing costs. Not included: appraisal fees, title insurance, or prepaid items.

faq.question: Is the APR Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the APR Calculator?
sections.body: The APR Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your loan amount, origination fees, stated interest rate, you get instant results including true apr, monthly payment, total cost of loan. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting true apr right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your loan amount and need to find the right true apr. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Loan Amount ($)

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: origination fees, stated interest rate, term. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your true apr, monthly payment, total cost of loan. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your loan amount before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
